title: National Provident Fund (Preservation and Disposal of Records) Regulations, 1967
title_en: National Provident Fund (Preservation and Disposal of Records) Regulations, 1967

provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the National Provident Fund (Preservation and Disposal of Records) Regulations.

All National Provident Fund records which fall within the following categories are hereby classified for the purpose of the National Archives Act, as Public Archives— (a) General policy and Correspondence files (subject to review). (b) Statistical and general report and publications.

The Public Archives classified under regulation 2 shall be disposed of or preserved as the case may be, in accordance with the Schedule to these Regulations. Schedule Type of Records Disposal Preservation 1. Departmental Records (Registry): (a) Policy and Correspondence files by subject To Records Centre one year after closure 1 1 P. (After review of individual files). (b) Personal Files To Records Centre one year after termination of service 2 2 D. 10 years. (c) Regulations and Instructions To Records Centre; convenient intervals 3 3 P. 2. Financial Records: (a) Computer printouts (employers' master file) To Records Centre at convenient intervals 4 4 P. (b) Computer printouts (current) Retain in N.P.F. office for six months 5 5 D. Six months after audit. (c) Accounts files containing N.P.F. 15, etc. To Records Centre one year after audit (the end of financial year) 6 6 D. 7 years. (d) Annual statement to members To Records Centre six months after date of issue 7 7 D. 7 years. 3. Records relating to Members of the fund: (a) Member's Records Cards (N.P.F.3) Retain in N.P.F. 8 8 Provisionally P., subject to review - possible use of microfilm. (b) Benefit files To Records Centre three years after closure of membership 9 9 D. 5 years 10 10 P. Sample periods including 1st 2 years; subject to statistics being abstracted. 4. Records relating to employers (see also 2(a) and 5(a)): (a) Employers' files To Records Centre three years after closure 11 11 D. 5 years. 12 12 P. Sample periods including first 2 years; subject to statistics being abstracted. 5 Compliance Records (see also 1(c) and 4(a)): (a) Reports of compliance officers To Records Centre at convenient intervals 13 13 P. 1 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 2 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 3 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 4 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 5 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 6 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 7 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 8 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 9 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 10 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 11 Classified as fit for destruction after time indicated. 12 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives. 13 Classified as for permanent preservation as Public Archives.
